ground_truth_source: package registry current version (npm / PyPI / crates)
method: For each library: LiveDocs `latest_version` (1 call) and context7 `resolve-library-id` (part of its resolve->query flow). `ground_truth` is the registry's current version at capture time. `livedocs.answer` is verbatim. `context7.answer` describes its TOP-RANKED (highest benchmark score) default match and the version that match reflects; `context7.is_current` is a human judgment of whether that default answer matches the current release. Reproduce by re-running both MCP tools.
honesty_note: Freshness only. context7 is a coverage-ranked doc/snippet retriever, not a version API; it wins on doc breadth and frequently carries the current version in a lower-ranked entry (conceded per library below). We do not measure coverage.

context7.answer: top match `/reactjs/react.dev` (benchmark 88.51) pinned to **v18**; v19.2.7 exists only in the lower-ranked `/react/react`
context7.note: default is a major version behind; current exists but not the top result

context7.answer: top match `/vitejs/vite` lists up to **v8.0.10** — one minor behind 8.1.3
context7.note: correct major, one minor behind; closest of the six

context7.answer: top match `/websites/fastapi_tiangolo` is versionless; best version-tagged entry `/fastapi/fastapi` tops out at **0.128.0**
top matches carry no version; the only version-tagged entry is **v1.10** (a major behind)
context7.note: no current-version signal in the default result

context7.answer: top match `/websites/rs_tokio` is versionless; the tagged entry is **1.49.0** — behind
top match `/websites/serde_rs` is versionless; no entry pinned to 1.0.228
context7.note: no current-version signal in the default result
